title: Mobile Development - 50 Words
definition: 50 essential terms for building applications for phones and tablets.

words.meaning: An app built specifically for one platform using its own language and tools.
words.sentence: We built a native app for iOS using Swift.

words.meaning: An app built to run on multiple operating systems from one codebase.
words.sentence: This cross-platform app runs on both Android and iOS.

words.meaning: An app combining native components with web technology.
words.sentence: The hybrid app uses a web view for some screens.

words.meaning: A digital distribution platform for downloading mobile applications.
words.sentence: We submitted our app to the app store for review.

words.meaning: A message sent to a user's device even when the app isn't open.
words.sentence: We sent a push notification about the flash sale.

words.meaning: A toolkit for developing applications for a specific mobile platform.
words.sentence: We used the mobile SDK to integrate the analytics tool.

words.meaning: Authorization a user grants an app to access device features or data.
words.sentence: The app requests camera permission to scan documents.

words.meaning: A user interface that adapts to different screen sizes and orientations.
words.sentence: The responsive UI adjusts smoothly between phone and tablet layouts.

words.meaning: The stages an app goes through, from launch to termination.
words.sentence: We save user data during the app lifecycle's background stage.

words.meaning: Software that simulates a mobile device for testing apps on a computer.
words.sentence: I tested the app on an emulator before trying a real device.

words.meaning: An unexpected termination of an app due to an error.
words.sentence: The app crash was caused by a null reference error.

words.meaning: App functionality available without an internet connection.
words.sentence: Offline mode lets users view previously loaded content without Wi-Fi.

words.meaning: A packaged format containing all resources needed to publish an app.
words.sentence: We upload an app bundle to the store instead of a raw APK.

words.meaning: Android Package Kit - the file format used to distribute Android apps.
words.sentence: You can install the app manually using the APK file.

words.meaning: The process where an app store evaluates an app before approval.
words.sentence: Our app passed the app store review after a small fix.

words.meaning: A purchase made within a mobile app for extra content or features.
words.sentence: Users can unlock premium features through an in-app purchase.

words.meaning: A link that opens a specific page within a mobile app.
words.sentence: The deep link takes users directly to the product page in the app.

words.meaning: How well an app or website adapts to mobile screen sizes.
words.sentence: We improved mobile responsiveness for smaller devices.

words.meaning: The initial experience guiding new users through an app's features.
words.sentence: The app onboarding walks new users through the main features.

words.meaning: A touch-based interaction like swiping or pinching used to control an app.
words.sentence: Swipe left is the gesture used to delete an item.

words.meaning: The graphic representing an app on a device's home screen.
words.sentence: We redesigned the app icon for the new branding.

words.meaning: Techniques used to reduce an app's power consumption.
words.sentence: Battery optimization reduced background location tracking frequency.

words.meaning: Tools tracking how users interact with a mobile app.
words.sentence: Mobile analytics showed most users drop off at the payment screen.

words.meaning: A notification scheduled and triggered directly by the app itself.
words.sentence: We set a local notification to remind users about their appointment.

words.meaning: Improving an app's visibility and ranking within app store search results.
words.sentence: App store optimization increased our downloads by improving keywords.

words.meaning: A screen design that adjusts automatically to fit different device sizes.
words.sentence: The responsive layout rearranges elements for smaller screens.

words.meaning: A task an app runs while not actively in the foreground.
words.sentence: A background process syncs data even when the app is minimized.

words.meaning: Software that automatically logs and reports app crashes.
words.sentence: The crash reporting tool alerted us within minutes of the issue.

words.meaning: Designing for mobile devices before adapting to larger screens.
words.sentence: We follow a mobile-first design approach for all new features.

words.meaning: The area on screen a user taps to trigger an action.
words.sentence: We enlarged the touch target so buttons are easier to tap.

words.meaning: A new version of an app released with fixes or features.
words.sentence: The latest app update fixes the login bug.

words.meaning: Releasing an app to a limited group before the full public launch.
words.sentence: We ran beta testing with 100 users before the public release.

words.meaning: The page describing an app in an app store, including screenshots and description.
words.sentence: We updated the app store listing with new screenshots.

words.meaning: Whether a device's display is in portrait or landscape mode.
words.sentence: The app locks screen orientation to portrait mode.

words.meaning: The wide variety of device types, screen sizes, and OS versions to support.
words.sentence: Android's device fragmentation makes thorough testing essential.

words.meaning: A popup asking the user to grant an app access to a feature.
words.sentence: The app permissions dialog asks for location access on first launch.

words.meaning: A small app extension displayed on a device's home screen.
words.sentence: We built a widget showing the weather without opening the app.

words.meaning: An introductory screen shown briefly while an app loads.
words.sentence: The splash screen displays our logo while the app initializes.

words.meaning: Verifying an app functions correctly across devices and conditions.
words.sentence: Mobile app testing covers different screen sizes and OS versions.

words.meaning: Isolating an app's data and processes from other apps for security.
words.sentence: App sandboxing prevents apps from accessing each other's private data.

words.meaning: An image that adapts in size and resolution based on the device.
words.sentence: Responsive images keep load times fast on smaller devices.

words.meaning: Measuring how users interact with deep links within an app.
words.sentence: Deep link tracking shows which campaign drove the most app opens.

words.meaning: The average score users give an app in an app store.
words.sentence: Our app store rating improved after fixing the crash bug.

words.meaning: The overall experience a user has while interacting with a mobile app.
words.sentence: We redesigned the checkout flow to improve mobile UX.

words.meaning: Keeping user data consistent across multiple devices.
words.sentence: Cross-device sync lets users continue where they left off on another phone.

words.meaning: Adapting an app's content and language for different regions.
words.sentence: App localization added support for five new languages.

words.meaning: Revealing information gradually to avoid overwhelming users on small screens.
words.sentence: Progressive disclosure hides advanced settings until the user needs them.

words.meaning: The process of sending a completed app for review and publishing.
words.sentence: We completed the app store submission ahead of the launch date.

words.meaning: Analyzing an app's speed and resource usage to identify bottlenecks.
words.sentence: Performance profiling revealed the image loading was slowing the app down.

words.meaning: Visual or haptic response confirming a user's touch interaction registered.
words.sentence: We added responsive touch feedback so buttons feel more interactive.

quiz.question: What is a 'native app'?
options: An app built specifically for one platform
An app that only works offline
A website disguised as an app
An app without a user interface
quiz.answer: An app built specifically for one platform
quiz.explanation: Native apps are built using platform-specific tools and languages.

quiz.question: What is 'deep linking'?
options: A link that opens a specific page within an app
quiz.answer: A link that opens a specific page within an app
quiz.explanation: Deep links direct users to specific in-app content.

quiz.question: What does APK stand for?
quiz.explanation: APK is the file format used to distribute Android applications.

quiz.question: A ___ screen is shown briefly while an app loads. (introductory)
quiz.explanation: A splash screen appears during app initialization.

quiz.question: What is 'app store optimization' (ASO)?
options: Improving an app's visibility in app store search results
Testing an app on multiple devices
quiz.answer: Improving an app's visibility in app store search results
quiz.explanation: ASO improves how easily users can discover an app in app stores.
